title: Google I/O 2025 – T41OD37tI54
text:
[MUSIC PLAYING] NEETHI THOMAS: Hi,
everyone, I'm Neethi, and I'm joined by Rejane. We're both group
product managers at Google Play
dedicated to helping you maximize your revenue. Today, we're excited to walk you
through a range of new features designed to amplify your
impact across critical phases of your app's journey. First, we will dive into
powerful new monetization tools focused on
boosting your bottom line and providing
greater flexibility. You'll learn about enhancements
like streamlined checkout experiences for one-time
products and subscriptions, expanded control over the
subscription lifecycle to help reduce churn,
and increased visibility for your subscription benefits. All these updates are
designed to empower you to offer more flexible
choices to your users and to optimize your
revenue streams. Then I'll show you the
ways Google Play is investing in the performance and
trustworthiness of our platform. You'll learn more about
how we have focused on making payments
easier and more secure for your global audience, how
we're optimizing the purchase experience through
AI-powered payment method selection and optimization,
card abandoned reminders, and purchase
flow recommendations, and, finally, how we're
enhancing pricing flexibility and bolstering our efforts to
mitigate fraud and abuse, all to build a trusted ecosystem
for you and your users. We're confident these new
features will provide you with immediate value
and help you achieve powerful results over time. So let's get started. REJANE FRANCA: As
Google Play, we're deeply committed to helping
you grow and succeed. We have heard directly
from many of you that maximizing revenue and
retaining valuable users are top priorities. To achieve this, it's
important to provide flexibility and a seamless
experience to your users. And that's why we
have been hard at work developing a comprehensive
suite of new features. They are designed to empower
you in several key ways. First, by offering more
dynamic and flexible ways to monetize through
new products,

title: Google I/O 2025 – T41OD37tI54
text:
And that's why we
have been hard at work developing a comprehensive
suite of new features. They are designed to empower
you in several key ways. First, by offering more
dynamic and flexible ways to monetize through
new products, like single checkout
for multiple products for both subscriptions
and one-time products. Second, by giving you greater
control over the subscription lifecycle with expanded
options for grace periods and account hold, tools
to help optimize renewals and increase the visibility
of your subscription benefits, and, finally, by providing you
with deeper insights and more efficiency through
expanded API access, redesigned Play Console
surfaces, precise tax management tools, and proactive, actionable
recommendations delivered directly to your inbox. So let's dive into how
these new features can empower you by offering more
dynamic and flexible ways to monetize. We will start by looking at how
we're enhancing the purchase experience for one-time
products with the introduction of single checkout. You can now bundle
several one-time products in a seamless combined
checkout flow, helping you to create
attractive offers and promotions for your users and making
it even more convenient for them to complete
their purchase. Bundling also enables you
to offer complementary items or discounts on bundles
to reward and incentivize your users, offering them
more choices than ever. This is a powerful way to
increase the overall visibility of your catalog and drive
sales of individual products by showcasing items
your users may not have considered otherwise. Building on the theme
of how providing more flexibility and choice, we
have also turned our attention to the world of subscriptions. As one of the world's largest
subscription platforms, with over a quarter of
a billion subscriptions, Google Play is
committed to making the subscriptions you
offer both easy to use and flexible to your user needs. We're excited to showcase
some of our new tools to help you accelerate
your monetization strategy
speaker: unknown

title: Google I/O 2025 – T41OD37tI54
text:
Google Play is
committed to making the subscriptions you
offer both easy to use and flexible to your user needs. We're excited to showcase
some of our new tools to help you accelerate
your monetization strategy and offer dynamic, flexible
options to your buyers. That's why we have extended
multiproduct checkout to subscriptions. Now you can unlock more pricing,
flexibility, and user choice by selling subscription
add-ons to base subscriptions with an aligned
payment schedule. Everything is combined
into one subscription to make it easy for
users to complete the purchase with a
streamlined payment process. For example, say you are a
generative AI app offering a basic AI subscription,
and now you see an opportunity to
increase your revenue by offering add-ons such as
writing or creative assist. During the checkout
process, your customer can see all the individual
items, both the base subscription and any
add-ons displayed in a single cart with an
aligned payment schedule. You can then decide how your
users upgrade or downgrade and add or remove add-ons. And users can manage payments
with more simplicity, as if they had a single
autorenewing subscription with aligned billing dates-- one charge, one purchase,
and everything stays in sync. So now that you
are able to offer your users an easy, seamless way
to manage their subscriptions, we also want to
give you even more control into how you offer
grace periods and account holds. You can now choose any
grace period up to 30 days and account hold
period up to 60 days, with a combined duration
between 30 and 60 days. Not only does this
give you more options. Users will then benefit from
a longer recovery period. And the best part-- giving your users
that flexibility can significantly reduce
your overall churn. In an earlier experiment,
developers who adopted this feature saw, on average, a 10%
reduction in involuntary churn for renews, with a 60-day
decline of recovery period. Speaking of renewals,
we understand that they are critical touch
points in the subscription

title: Google I/O 2025 – T41OD37tI54
text:
feature saw, on average, a 10%
reduction in involuntary churn for renews, with a 60-day
decline of recovery period. Speaking of renewals,
we understand that they are critical touch
points in the subscription lifecycle. Our data shows that
70% of subscribers cancel due to a
perceived lack of value, often citing costs
or insufficient use. As a reminder, you can find
these cancellation responses and more insights
on Play Console to help you understand why
users might be leaving. We have also invested in tools
that give you more control and help you effectively
reduce overall churn during these crucial periods. For example,
notifications now remind users of the value of
their subscriptions at critical times during
the renewal period, like at the end
of the intraperiod or during monthly
billing periods. And to make the renewal
experience even more seamless and proactively help
prevent failed payments, we now notify users when
their primary payment method is invalid and
encourage them to update it before the renewal date. And finally, you can also
instantly test subscription renewal flows in the
Play Billing Lab app, significantly reducing the
previous 30-minute delay. These new features empower you
to optimize your subscription renewal process and
retain valuable users. To make sure that
your users understand and fully use all of their
subscription benefits, we're also showcasing all of
your subscription benefits in even more places. We have expanded visibility
across key touchpoints in the user journey, including
within the subscription center and purchase flows,
during cancellation, in reminder emails, and other
surfaces across the Play Store. Don't miss out on the chance
to unlock more customer flexibility, choice, and reduce
churn with these new features. Explore how they
can help you today by using the link on our screen. While providing customer
flexibility and choice and minimizing churn
are key, we also know that truly unlocking
growth on Google Play requires a deep understanding
of your business.

title: Google I/O 2025 – T41OD37tI54
text:
by using the link on our screen. While providing customer
flexibility and choice and minimizing churn
are key, we also know that truly unlocking
growth on Google Play requires a deep understanding
of your business. To empower you with
this understanding, we've been working
on new features that enhance data access and give
you more control and greater efficiency, enabling you
to make informed decisions and optimize your strategies
for maximum impact. To give you immediate
access to all the details, including subscription
pricing phases, we are expanding
access to Orders API. This allows for more
efficient analysis, faster decision-making, and
the ability to implement more sophisticated strategies
for customer management and engagement-- all the data you need
instantly at your fingertips. And to provide you with
better data visibility, we are introducing
brand new services on Console, designed to
highlight all your top metrics and understand how your
app is doing at a glance. This empowers you to quickly
identify trends, understand user behavior, and make informed
decisions to optimize your app's growth and monetization. The redesigned
app dashboard also improves efficiency by
putting common objectives front and center. You will see your data grouped
into four common tasks-- test and release,
monitor and improve, grow users, and
monetize with Play. To empower you to dig
deeper into these areas, we're also launching
new overview pages for all of those tasks. These pages help you have
more insight into your data by specifically focusing
on each of the four most common objectives-- test and
release, monitor and improve, growth, and monetization. This way, you can stay on top
of your lifecycle of your app and make more informed decisions
by seeing insights related to release rollout status,
app installs, first opens, engagement, revenue,
buyer data, and more. And to give you more
control over your pricing, we're now adding more
product classifications for even more precise and
granular tax configurations.

title: Google I/O 2025 – T41OD37tI54
text:
to release rollout status,
app installs, first opens, engagement, revenue,
buyer data, and more. And to give you more
control over your pricing, we're now adding more
product classifications for even more precise and
granular tax configurations. Plus, we're simplifying and
streamlining our tax settings for better visibility, no matter
where you are or what you sell. This helps give
you more efficiency in managing your business
across different regions and helps ensure accurate
pricing for users. To help you stay on top
of all these new features and gain proactive insights
into your business performance, we have also integrated growth
and technical recommendations directly into your
Play Console inbox. These alerts will notify
you of potential issues, like currency fluctuations
or integration errors, allowing you to
take swift action. For example, if
you're not currently using server-side
purchase acknowledgment and real-time developer
notifications, you could be missing
valuable sales due to network
issues or purchases made outside of your app. You can learn how to
make your Play billing integration even more effective
with our Code Lab on the link below. Or you can also check
your Play Console inbox to see other recommendations,
such as for price adjustments. These powerful
monetization tools are just the beginning
of how Google Play supporting your growth. Explore these tools
today to unlock your business total ROI and
revenue growth potential. Next, Neethi is
going to walk you through all of the ways
Google Play is investing in the performance
of our platform and the ways we help you
build trust with your users. NEETHI THOMAS: Thanks, Rejane. At Google Play, we're
committed to helping you build your business
sustainably on a platform you and your users trust. Whether it's a wide
range of payment options and local currencies,
recommendations tailored to your audience,
or all the ways we protect your users
from fraud and abuse, Play is committed
to being a trusted and high-performance
ecosystem that you

title: Google I/O 2025 – T41OD37tI54
text:
Whether it's a wide
range of payment options and local currencies,
recommendations tailored to your audience,
or all the ways we protect your users
from fraud and abuse, Play is committed
to being a trusted and high-performance
ecosystem that you can operate in with confidence. First, let's start with
the ways we're making it easier, more secure, and
more seamless for your users to complete payments. With over 2.5 billion users
across 190-plus markets, Google Play offers
unparalleled global reach. We already support a wide
range of payment options, including traditional credit
cards and over 300 local forms of payments in 65-plus markets. But as you know, the
payment landscape changes rapidly,
so we're constantly staying on top of global
trends, new forms of payment, and adding popular
payment methods to meet evolving user needs. Over the last year, we've
increased our local payment method coverage in key markets,
including Pix in Brazil, Cash App in the US,
and BLIK in Poland. And this year, we've
launched even more. We've added TROY in
Turkey, Airtel in Kenya, PayPal in South Africa,
and NAPAS in Vietnam. And that's not all. We're also expanding more ways
users can pay by launching QRIS in Indonesia, a new QR code
standard that enables seamless cashless payments. And coming soon, we'll be
one of the first platforms to offer Pix
Automatico in Brazil. We've partnered with
the Brazilian government to offer this new feature that
enables automatic payments for subscriptions using Pix,
with broad availability coming later this year. On top of a wide range
of payment methods, we also believe it's important
to offer local currencies that your users are
already familiar with to make it easier for
them to complete purchases with confidence. That's why this year, we've
enabled local currency support in Senegal, Cameroon, Cote
d'Ivoire, and Mongolia. And as always, we'll handle all
the global currency complexities for you, simplifying all your
international transactions so you can focus on
what you do best--
speaker: unknown

title: Google I/O 2025 – T41OD37tI54
text:
in Senegal, Cameroon, Cote
d'Ivoire, and Mongolia. And as always, we'll handle all
the global currency complexities for you, simplifying all your
international transactions so you can focus on
what you do best-- building amazing games and apps. To match changing
regional payment norms, we're also expanding "ask
someone to pay" to more regions. This feature enables
users to request purchases from friends and family
outside their Google family group via payment links. It was first launched
in India and is now rolling out in the US,
Japan, Indonesia, and Mexico. And finally, to help more
buyers be purchase ready, we've been proactively
encouraging people to set up payment methods in
advance, both within the Play Store and during
Android device setup, and even during Google
account creation. This has boosted global
conversion rates by up to 3% and added 7% more
buyers year-to-date, resulting in higher revenues
for your apps and games. The efforts over the past year
has resulted in a 240 million increase in global
consumer spending from people completing
more purchases with the more
frictionless experience, translating directly to
higher revenue for you from your apps and games. On top of the payment
methods we support and all the diverse range
of options we offer, we also continue to invest in
purchase optimizations that work quietly in the
background to help you achieve powerful business results. First up, our AI models, trained
on billions of transactions, present the right payment
method at the right time and optimize payment
authorizations. These optimizations help
maximize conversion and lifetime value for your users,
and they've already driven incredible results. Over the last year, we've seen
an increase of 200 million additional consumer spending. In addition, users can now
manage multiple payment backup methods at their
Google account level instead of having to add one for
each individual subscription. This feature simplifies
subscription management for the user and help
you reduce churn.
speaker: unknown

title: Google I/O 2025 – T41OD37tI54
text:
In addition, users can now
manage multiple payment backup methods at their
Google account level instead of having to add one for
each individual subscription. This feature simplifies
subscription management for the user and help
you reduce churn. We've started rolling
it out in the US, and it has already shown
early results, including a 5% increase in
successful renewals in the US for eligible users. Beyond AI, we continuously
simplify the entire checkout experience. We've seen developers drive
more app re-engagement with automated cart abandoned
reminders in the store for one-time purchases. So now we're extending
this to subscriptions. By combining features like
this with other optimizations, developers can significantly
boost their revenue. Our early results show
that these optimizations have increased global
consumer spending by over half a billion dollars. Besides a more
seamless checkout flow, we're also investing in
providing better product recommendations during
contextual moments with purchase flow recommendations. This feature helps
you guide users towards other relevant purchases
at just the right moments in their journey,
like when they've just bought something similar,
or if they're just about to abandon their cart. Last year, we launched
purchase flow recommendations as an early access program
and saw incredible results. We're excited to
announce that we have now made purchase flow
recommendations generally available in the Console. Over 400 apps have already
set this feature up and are seeing increased
conversions and spend. Setting this feature
up is straightforward, with only a few simple
steps in the Console to select eligible SKUs. So don't miss out on
an easy opportunity to boost your conversions and
explore this feature today. To support your revenue growth
even further, starting today, we're increasing pricing
flexibility and allowing products with pricing
up to $5,000 USD, or the local equivalent. This feature is
available upon request,
speaker: unknown

title: Google I/O 2025 – T41OD37tI54
text:
To support your revenue growth
even further, starting today, we're increasing pricing
flexibility and allowing products with pricing
up to $5,000 USD, or the local equivalent. This feature is
available upon request, and you can apply using the
link shown on the screen. Finally, as always,
we're continuing to enhance the Play ecosystem
with features to help mitigate fraud and abuse. We've expanded pricing arbitrage
protection to more countries, and we're automatically
detecting and blocking abusive purchases that exploit
regional price differences. This empowers you
to confidently offer localized pricing and promotions
with features designed to help safeguard your revenue. For limited
availability content, we're providing granular
control with payment location restrictions in
the Play Console, and we've enhanced protection
against free trial interpricing abuse for subscriptions. That's a lot of features. So when it comes to optimizing
the entire purchase experience, simplifying payments, and
expanding global reach, we strive to be as comprehensive
as possible to meet your needs. Using any of these
features on their own can help maximize your overall
ROI, but when used together, they can help you achieve
the most powerful results. Learn how developers
like The Hindu have used our features to
increase their subscriber base by 65%. By shifting their monetization
model from one-time products to auto-renewing subscriptions,
offering their active users a more frictionless
purchase experience, and implementing improved
user authentication, The Hindu has seen
a massive increase in subscription
rates and revenue. Check out their success story
using the link on the screen. REJANE FRANCA: Thanks, Neethi. To fully unlock the power
of Play Billing platform, including the new tools and
features announced today, we strongly recommend upgrading
to Play Billing Library 8.0, available at the end of June. As a reminder, we support each
major Billing Library release for two years. Support for version 6
ends in November 2025,

title: Google I/O 2025 – T41OD37tI54

we strongly recommend upgrading
to Play Billing Library 8.0, available at the end of June. As a reminder, we support each
major Billing Library release for two years. Support for version 6
ends in November 2025, and upgrades are required for

building to help you grow your business on Google Play. We will continue
to partner with you to learn more about your needs,
offer flexible monetization solutions for your
apps and games, and support your
growth along the way. To learn more about
